pages.raw_text:
At least a dozen unions now
meeting in places scattered in var.
ious sections of the city will likely
make their headquarters on the
third floor of the Pantages theater
building. ]. f. Eastman, an enter
prising plumber, has secured a
three year lease on this hall. 1t has
already been chosen as a meeting
place for the revived building
trades council, and it is Mr.. Bash
mans intention to try to induce
practically every union in the build
ing trades to rent the hall for their
particular weekly or semi-monthly
business meetings.


A great revival in unionism is on
in Spokane. After lying dormant
for months. that part of organized
labor's work dependent upon the
united efforts of the business
agents of the various unions is to
be on in full force once again. Reg
ular morning meetings of business
agents are to be held again within
a short time. Spokane union men
feel that considerable benefit has
been foregone and more than a
little has been lost to them in the
last few months by the business
agents failure to meet daily to dis
cuss matters arising in the various
organizations affiliated with the
